title: Understand and Navigate the Recruitment Process

sections.heading: UQ Business, Economics and Law Faculty - Recruitment Ready Program
sections.body:
Recruitment Ready helps Business, Economics and Law (BEL) students understand real-world recruitment processes and build the skills needed for competitive applications. Delivered every semester in collaboration with an industry partner, the program gives you hands-on experience in how employers assess candidates for internships, vacation programs and graduate roles.

The Recruitment Ready program will help you develop practical insights into:

- what to expect from a real recruitment process
- the behaviours, attributes and professional skills employers look for
- how to stand out and communicate your value confidently
- how to present professionally, in writing and in person
- how to reflect on feedback and improve for future applications

Over just a few weeks, you'll gain a clear understanding of the recruitment process - from submitting applications to interviewing - while practising the skills employers value most. The program is designed for mid-degree students and is best completed before applying for work experience or employment opportunities.

sections.heading: Navigate the Recruitment Process
sections.body:
Applying for graduate roles can be exciting but it can also feel complex and overwhelming. Knowing how the process works will help you stay organised and improve your chances of success.

In this module you'll learn how to:

- understand the difference between graduate programs and graduate roles
- recognise the key stages of the recruitment process
- track and manage your job applications.
